g.text: not yet supported (v1 = azure-container-apps only); stop
[step 3 — ambiguity OR a GENERALIZED landmine (any choice whose correctness
depends on a fact NOT grounded in the inputs: app-config / purpose / access-path)]
SURFACE the gap, never bury it as a runbook STOP note
step 4 — MANDATORY dialect confirmation, BEFORE any artifact (USE-DEPLOY-DIALECT)
resolve requested IaC dialect (bicep | terraform) — exactly one
— NO silent default; never assume prod- or dev-grade
Purpose RECOMMENDS the sizing/posture; the operator
confirms. Secure-by-default is preserved — the vault
stays most-restrictive; any relaxation is explicit.
step 6 — ACCESS-COMPLETENESS pass, BEFORE any artifact (USE-DEPLOY-ACCESS-CHECK)
[each secured store (Key Vault, DB, ACR, remote state)]
name READER + WRITER; each needs an identity
and a network path the chosen posture ADMITS
[read-but-never-written, OR a writer with no admitted path]
BLOCKING: SURFACE the hole + connectivity options
The diagram is not "done" until every secured store has
BOTH a reader and a writer with an admitted path. A
complete-looking diagram with a missing write path is
exactly the failure this gate prevents.
step 7 — write diagram.puml; show BOTH the runtime read path
AND the provisioning write path; doc-only stereotypes
Step 8 — FULL PARITY regardless of dialect: Container
Apps, ACR, Azure DB for PostgreSQL (TLS; tier set by
bypass=AzureServices when peers private) + BOTH role
grants (reader Secrets User, writer Secrets Officer),
secret refs, ingress 8080, pre-traffic migration job.
Hardening that creates a new requirement surfaces the
step 9 — write runbook.md (numbered steps; STOP before every
state-changing or go-live step; secret step states both preconditions)
identically for either dialect. Caveat: migrations-
step 11 — report written files; review the diagram
and run the runbook yourself. Executes NOTHING.
